title: OpenLLM Metrics Alert Event v1
description: F033 canonical alert event consumed by the notifier worker. Producers include the Alertmanager webhook receiver (Phase 1 alerts: F012/F027) and the future F030 policy-evaluator worker. The notifier matches against (tenant_id, severity, source), fans out to configured channels, and records each attempt in notification_deliveries.

id.description: Globally unique alert event identifier. Acts as the idempotency key for notification_deliveries (UNIQUE on (alert_event_id, channel_id)).

tenant_id.description: Tenant UUID (string form). Every alert belongs to exactly one tenant; cross-tenant fan-out is not permitted.

severity.description: Severity bucket. Routing rules match against this enum.

source.description: Producer-supplied source tag. Examples: 'alertmanager', 'slo', 'quota', 'anomaly', 'policy', 'drift'. Routing rules can match an allowlist of sources.

summary.description: Short human-readable summary suitable for the subject line of an email or the title of a webhook payload.

description.description: Long-form description. Must not contain LLM prompt or completion content (F033 README §11).

labels.description: Opaque k/v labels (team, app, env, provider, model, route, etc.). Values are forwarded verbatim into both webhook payloads and email bodies. Producers MUST NOT place secrets here.

started_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp at which the underlying condition started firing.

ended_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp at which the condition resolved. Optional; omitted while the alert is firing.

resource.description: Optional resource identifier the alert refers to (e.g. 'provider=openai,model=gpt-4o,team=platform-search').

runbook_url.description: Optional URL to a remediation runbook. Included verbatim in delivered payloads.
